findings.title: Liability cap at 12 months — YELLOW under our guide, despite being market standard
findings.clause: shall not exceed the fees paid or payable by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim
findings.rationale: Our classification guide sets GREEN at a cap of twenty-four (24) months of fees or higher — a deliberately buyer-side floor priced for vendor concentration and our claimant posture. A cap of at least 12 but below 24 months is YELLOW and needs head-of-legal sign-off. Meridian's 12-month mutual cap sits exactly on the YELLOW floor. A 12-month cap is common vendor paper, but market practice does not set the classification — the guide does. Recommend countering to 24 months; fall back to YELLOW-with-sign-off only if Meridian is fungible.

findings.title: Net 20 payment terms — RED under our guide
findings.clause: Customer shall pay all undisputed invoices within twenty (20) days of the invoice date
findings.rationale: The fees-payment guide is explicit: net twenty (20) days or shorter is RED. Our consolidated payables cycle is built for net forty-five (45) or longer (GREEN); the YELLOW band runs net twenty-one (21) to net forty-four (44) and only with a 1.5% early-payment discount. Net 20 sits exactly on the RED boundary, and the position's rationale is operational, not cosmetic — terms our payables cycle cannot reliably meet convert ordinary processing time into breach and late-fee exposure. Counter to net 45; do not soften this to negotiable.

findings.title: Customer termination for convenience on 30 days' notice — GREEN
findings.clause: Customer may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to Provider
findings.rationale: The termination-renewal guide marks customer termination for convenience on thirty (30) days' notice or less as GREEN — this clause sits exactly on the GREEN boundary. Exit speed is the counterweight to vendor dependence in our standards, and this clause delivers it as drafted. No change requested.
